current.summary: Pragmatic review of hooks.md spec complete; confirmed modular structure needed; locked in on real code reference implementations (no pseudocode), W3C-style document split

activeWork.description: Break hooks.md from monolithic spec into 7 modular documents; implement Python + TypeScript reference implementations for conformance
activeWork.progress: Completed pragmatic review. Confirmed spec is well-designed but needs modularization. Validated: (1) Section 8 blocking matrix as living doc (GitHub Action automation), (2) JSON merge model, (3) Reference impls must be real code not pseudocode. Locked in W3C-style modular structure (core spec + 6 separate docs).

git.lastCommit: 6f07743 bd sync: 2026-04-08 conflict detection research + handoff

recentDecisions: hooks.md modularization: split into core spec (~400 lines), events.md, tools.md, capabilities.md, blocking-matrix.md, provider-strengths.md, keep existing glossary/policy/security/schema/test-vectors
Reference implementations: Python + TypeScript, real runnable code with tests (no pseudocode). Covers: manifest parsing, exit code resolution, bare string matchers, one provider event/tool mapping
Section 8 blocking matrix: designed as living document, automated GitHub Action updates twice daily against provider documentation
Confirm hooks use JSON merge (same as MCP configs) — simpler than filesystem symlinks
Spec neutrality: never mention syllago in interchange specs, they are community-neutral
nextSteps: Create modular document structure: split docs/spec/hooks/hooks.md into 7 separate markdown files
Implement Python reference implementation for core conformance kit
Implement TypeScript reference implementation (parallel to Python)
Update main hooks.md to link to modular docs and point to reference implementations
Plan GitHub Action for Section 8 blocking matrix automation (nightly provider doc checks)
Apply same modularization pattern to future interchange specs (skills, MCP, commands, etc.)

context.notes: Spec is not overengineered—it's comprehensive. Main issue was presentation (one 2000-line file). Modularization solves clarity without losing depth. Section 8 is the differentiator: automated provider doc checking prevents spec rot and keeps blocking behavior matrix authoritative. Reference implementations anchor conformance and show how to handle the trickier parts (degradation, capability negotiation, event/tool mapping). No new beads created yet—continue this session or hand off to next with execution plan ready.
